dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 3495 RH: Direct Seller and Real Estate Agent Harmonization Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Kiley of California (for himself and Mr. Cuellar) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce

Additional sponsors: Mr. Carbajal, Mr. Van Drew, Mr. Fong, Mr. Suozzi, Mr. Moore of Utah, Mr. Bergman, Mr. Moran, Mr. Alford, Mr. Sherman, Mr. Moolenaar, Mr. Costa, Mr. Hudson, Mr. Mann, Mr. Cole, Mr. Valadao, Mrs. McClain, Mr. Garcia of California, Mr. Owens, Mrs. Kim, Mr. Swalwell, Mr. Messmer, Ms. Scholten, Mr. Calvert, Mr. Soto, Mr. Thompson of California, Mr. Baumgartner, Mr. McGuire, Mr. Crank, Mr. Finstad, and Mr. Harder of California
Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
action.action-instruction: Strike out all after the enacting clause and insert the part printed in italic
For text of introduced bill, see copy of bill as introduced on May 19, 2025

form.official-title: To amend the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to clarify the definition of employee as it relates to direct sellers and real estate agents, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Direct Seller and Real Estate Agent Harmonization Act.
Section 3(e) of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 (29 U.S.C. 203(e)) is amended by inserting after paragraph (5) the following:

paragraph.text: The term employee does not include any direct seller or qualified real estate agent (as such terms are defined in section 3508(b) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986).

endorsement.action-desc: Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
